manipulate.label: Determine Grid Expansion or Terrain Rotation
manipulate.code:
data.direction = [] data.exp_terrain_found = false data.expand_cols = nil data.expand_rows = nil data.expand_needed = false data.rotate_terrain_id = nil data.rotate_needed = false
player_pos = data.current_player["position"]
row = player_pos[0] col = player_pos[1]
cell = data.game_state[row-1][col-1]
if cell["exit"] if [1, 12].include?(row) || [1, 12].include?(col) terrain_id = cell["terrainID"] if terrain_id data.rotate_needed = true data.rotate_terrain_id = terrain_id else raise "Error: No terrainID found at player position!" end data.expand_needed = false else if [4,5,8,9].include?(row) if row == 4 expansion_rows = (5..8).to_a # Moving south data.direction << 0 elsif row == 5 expansion_rows = (1..4).to_a # Moving north data.direction << 3 elsif row == 8 expansion_rows = (9..12).to_a # Moving south data.direction << 0 elsif row == 9 expansion_rows = (5..8).to_a # Moving north data.direction << 3 end
data.direction << ((col - 1) % 4)
if col.between?(1,4) expansion_cols = (1..4).to_a elsif col.between?(5,8) expansion_cols = (5..8).to_a elsif col.between?(9,12) expansion_cols = (9..12).to_a end
else data.direction << ((row - 1) % 4) if [4,5,8,9].include?(col) if col == 4 expansion_cols = (5..8).to_a # Moving east data.direction << 0 elsif col == 5 expansion_cols = (1..4).to_a # Moving west data.direction << 3 elsif col == 8 expansion_cols = (9..12).to_a # Moving east data.direction << 0 elsif col == 9 expansion_cols = (5..8).to_a # Moving west data.direction << 3 end
if row.between?(1,4) expansion_rows = (1..4).to_a elsif row.between?(5,8) expansion_rows = (5..8).to_a elsif row.between?(9,12) expansion_rows = (9..12).to_a end else data.expand_needed = false end end
empty = true expansion_rows&.each do |r| expansion_cols&.each do |c| cell_to_check = data.game_state[r-1][c-1] if cell_to_check["terrainID"] empty = false break end end break unless empty end
if empty data.expand_needed = true data.expand_rows = expansion_rows data.expand_cols = expansion_cols else data.expand_needed = false end data.rotate_needed = false end end

manipulate.label: Find Suitable Terrain
manipulate.code:
def rotate_matrix(matrix, degrees) case degrees when 0 then matrix when 90 then matrix.transpose.map(&:reverse) when 180 then matrix.map(&:reverse).reverse when 270 then matrix.transpose.reverse end end
def rotate_walls(horizontal, vertical, degrees) case degrees when 0 [horizontal, vertical] when 90 [vertical.map(&:reverse), horizontal.reverse] when 180 [horizontal.reverse.map(&:reverse), vertical.reverse.map(&:reverse)] when 270 [vertical.reverse, horizontal.map(&:reverse)] end end
# ====== Setup ======
terrains = data.terrain_template terrains = JSON.parse(terrains) if terrains.is_a?(String)
used_ids = data.terrain_pieces.map { |t| t["id"] } available_terrains = terrains.reject { |t| used_ids.include?(t["id"]) }
# ====== Search for matching terrain ======
new_tile = nil new_rotation = nil found_match = false t_row = data.direction[0] t_col = data.direction[1]
available_terrains.shuffle.each do |candidate| [0, 90, 180, 270].each do |rotation| rotated_cells = rotate_matrix(candidate["cells"], rotation)
if rotated_cells[t_row][t_col] && rotated_cells[t_row][t_col]["exit"] == true new_tile = candidate new_rotation = rotation found_match = true break end end break if found_match end
unless found_match && new_tile data.exp_terrain_found = false return else data.exp_terrain_found = true end
# ====== Place the terrain ======
rotated_horizontal, rotated_vertical = rotate_walls(new_tile["horizontal_walls"], new_tile["vertical_walls"], new_rotation) rotated_cells_final = rotate_matrix(new_tile["cells"], new_rotation)
start_row = data.expand_rows.first start_col = data.expand_cols.first placement = ((start_row - 1) / 4 + 1) * 10 + ((start_col - 1) / 4 + 1)
data.current_terrain = { "id" => new_tile["id"], "home" => new_tile["home"], "rotation" => new_rotation, "placement" => placement, "horizontal_walls" => rotated_horizontal, "vertical_walls" => rotated_vertical }
data.terrain_pieces ||= [] data.terrain_pieces << data.current_terrain
grid = data.game_state
(0..3).each do |r| (0..3).each do |c| global_pos = [start_row + r, start_col + c] rotated_cell = rotated_cells_final[r][c]
grid_row = global_pos[0] - 1 grid_col = global_pos[1] - 1
grid[grid_row][grid_col]["terrainID"] = new_tile["id"] grid[grid_row][grid_col]["effect"] = rotated_cell["effect"] grid[grid_row][grid_col]["occupied"] = nil grid[grid_row][grid_col]["exit"] = rotated_cell["exit"] end end
data.game_state = grid

manipulate.label: Transpose Terrain 90 Degrees
manipulate.code:
require 'json'
# ====== Helpers ======
def rotate_matrix(matrix, degrees) case degrees when 0 then matrix when 90 then matrix.transpose.map(&:reverse) when 180 then matrix.map(&:reverse).reverse when 270 then matrix.transpose.reverse end end
def rotate_walls(horizontal, vertical, degrees) case degrees when 0 [horizontal, vertical] when 90 [vertical.map(&:reverse), horizontal.reverse] when 180 [horizontal.reverse.map(&:reverse), vertical.reverse.map(&:reverse)] when 270 [vertical.reverse, horizontal.map(&:reverse)] end end
def rotate_coord(row, col, degrees) case degrees when 0 then [row, col] when 90 then [col, 3 - row] when 180 then [3 - row, 3 - col] when 270 then [3 - col, row] end end
# ====== Rotate the Terrain Piece ======
# 1. Find the terrain to rotate terrain_to_rotate = data.terrain_pieces.find { |t| t["id"] == data.rotate_terrain_id }
# 2. Rotate its properties terrain_to_rotate["rotation"] = (terrain_to_rotate["rotation"] + 90) % 360
rotated_horizontal, rotated_vertical = rotate_walls( terrain_to_rotate["horizontal_walls"], terrain_to_rotate["vertical_walls"], 90 )
terrain_to_rotate["horizontal_walls"] = rotated_horizontal terrain_to_rotate["vertical_walls"] = rotated_vertical
# 3. Set current_terrain data.current_terrain = terrain_to_rotate.dup
# 4. Update terrain_pieces data.terrain_pieces.map! do |t| t["id"] == data.rotate_terrain_id ? terrain_to_rotate : t end
# ====== Rotate the Terrain Cells and Update Game State ======
# 5. Get starting row and col from placement placement = data.current_terrain["placement"]
row_start = ((placement / 10) - 1) * 4 col_start = ((placement % 10) - 1) * 4
# 6. Fetch original terrain template terrain_template = data.terrain_template.find { |t| t["id"] == data.current_terrain["id"] } raise "Terrain template not found!" unless terrain_template
# 7. Rotate the cells rotated_cells = rotate_matrix(terrain_template["cells"], data.current_terrain["rotation"])
# 8. Find players standing on the terrain before rotation players_to_relocate = []
(0..3).each do |r| (0..3).each do |c| global_r = row_start + r global_c = col_start + c cell = data.game_state[global_r][global_c] if cell["occupied"] players_to_relocate << { id: cell["occupied"], old_local: [r, c] } end end end
# 9. Update game state cells to the new rotated cells (0..3).each do |r| (0..3).each do |c| global_r = row_start + r global_c = col_start + c rotated_cell = rotated_cells[r][c]
data.game_state[global_r][global_c]["terrainID"] = data.current_terrain["id"] data.game_state[global_r][global_c]["effect"] = rotated_cell["effect"] data.game_state[global_r][global_c]["exit"] = rotated_cell["exit"] data.game_state[global_r][global_c]["occupied"] = nil end end
# 10. Move players to their rotated new positions players_to_relocate.each do |player_info| old_r, old_c = player_info[:old_local] new_r, new_c = rotate_coord(old_r, old_c, 90)
global_new_r = row_start + new_r global_new_c = col_start + new_c
# Update game_state data.game_state[global_new_r][global_new_c]["occupied"] = player_info[:id]
# Update player_data player = data.player_data.find { |p| p["id"] == player_info[:id] } if player player["position"] = [global_new_r + 1, global_new_c + 1] end
# Update current_player current = data.current_player if current && current["id"] == player_info[:id] current["position"] = [global_new_r + 1, global_new_c + 1] data.current_player = current end
# Update initiative_order if data.initiative_order data.initiative_order.each do |pl| next unless pl["id"] == player_info[:id] pl["position"] = [global_new_r + 1, global_new_c + 1] end end end

alternative.condition: data.manipulate == true && data.expand_needed == true && data.exp_terrain_found == false

default.title: #, the way is SHUT!
default.message: Try to find another way around the dungeon...

alternative.condition: (data.manipulate == true && data.expand_needed == true && data.exp_terrain_found == true) || data.manipulate == false

default.title: #, welcome to the Dungeon of Doom!
default.message: Please wait while the map is set up...

default.title: #, a new part of the dungeon is REVEALED!
default.message: Please stand clear of the robot...

default.title: #, the terrain is SHIFTING!
default.message: Please wait while the terrain piece is rotates...
